rulings.Case Title: MILES HENRY COBB SELLAI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, line 1, OTHER MICHELLE BAASS DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES' Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien Off calendar. The moving and opposing parties failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with local rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: JACKSON CEBULLA ET AL VS. CIVIC CENTER INN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdraw Responses To Requests For Admissions; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Ilan N. Rosen Janfaza, Esq.; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFFS JACKSON CEBULLA, PATRICIA FALCON-CEBULLA'S Motion To Withdraw Responses To Requests For Admissions 1. Court agrees with Defendants. 2. Plaintiffs' counsel has not established excusable neglect 3. Motion is also untimely. 4. Discovery has closed. 5. To grant motion at this late date would be highly prejudicial to the defendant 6. Motion/denied. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.

rulings.Case Title: NANCY WEBBER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Quash Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Northwestern Medicaine And Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF NANCY WEBBER'S MOTION To Quash Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Northwestern Medicaine And Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, Etc. Motion to Quash/granted in part. 1. CCSF's subpoenas, which properly seek medical information in this personal injury action, are overbroad. 2. They seek "all" medical and insurance records with no time limitation. (See Decl. Graziano, 6 Exh. B.) 3. Recognizing the overbreadth of the subpoenas and problems with withdrawing and reissuing them, the parties agreed that a First Look Agreement could resolve the discovery dispute. (See Decl. Graziano, 10 Exh. F.) 4. The Parties, however, could not agree on how long the review process would take. As a new trial date has been set the issue is now moot. 5. There is no longer an impediment to the parties implementing a First Look Agreement. 6. The Parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the precise scope of the First Look Agreement as contemplated in plaintiff's October 23, 2024, letter. (See Decl. Graziano, 7 Exh. C.) 7. The parties shall fashion the agreement by January 22, 2025. If issues remain outstanding, then either party may file a clarifying motion by January 29, 2025. 8. Request for sanctions/denied. The Court finds the parties acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)
